article_meta.title: Border czar Tom Homan on Thursday invited Catholic leadership to watch him and U.S

reasoning: The assistant repeatedly flagged quoted or clearly attributed statements (Homan’s quotes/paraphrases) as author bias, misapplied bias types, and missed actual author-prose instances like “even more heated” and potentially the loaded descriptor “Border czar.”

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All four flagged passages are either direct quotes or explicitly attributed to Homan (“He argued,” “He said,” “Homan said”), violating the attribution rule throughout.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Every flagged item centers on sourced/attributed content rather than the author’s own prose; none are genuine author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed clear author-prose bias such as the emotive/loaded phrasing “even more heated” and arguably the label “Border czar.”
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are misapplied: e.g., calling an attributed claim “Opinion Presented as Fact,” and “Omission of Source Attribution” despite explicit attribution.
